explanation:
**Normal health values** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. These are guidelines; your personal medical history always takes precedence.

## How It's Calculated

Maximum 2,300mg sodium daily (WHO). With hypertension: 1,500mg.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to any values outside what's reasonable.

## Normal Reference Values

| Parameter | Normal Range (Adults) |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Body Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C |
| Blood Oxygen (SpO2) | 95-100% |
| Fasting Glucose | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL Cholesterol | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL Cholesterol | >40 (Men) / >50 (Women) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is a guideline only. For critical decisions (medical, financial, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What's the recommended daily sodium intake?
faq.a: The WHO recommends a maximum of 2,300mg sodium per day for adults. Those with hypertension should aim for 1,500mg daily.

faq.q: How much sodium is too much per day?
faq.a: Exceeding 2,300mg daily (WHO standard) is considered excessive. For hypertension patients, exceeding 1,500mg daily increases health risks significantly.

faq.q: Does excess sodium cause high blood pressure?
faq.a: Yes. Consuming too much sodium can raise blood pressure and increase cardiovascular disease risk, especially in salt-sensitive individuals.

faq.q: How can I reduce my daily sodium intake?
faq.a: Choose fresh foods over processed ones, read nutrition labels carefully, limit salt in cooking, avoid processed meats, and reduce condiments and sauces.

faq.q: Do men and women need different sodium amounts?
faq.a: The WHO recommendations are the same for both (2,300mg max daily), but hypertension management may vary based on individual factors and medical history.
This is a guideline tool only. For medical diagnosis and personalized advice, consult a healthcare professional. Individual needs vary significantly.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about sodium intake?
faq.a: If your values are outside normal ranges, if you have symptoms like swelling or fatigue, or if you have a family history of cardiovascular disease.

faq.q: What sources does this calculator use?
faq.a: International organizations: WHO, NIH, CDC, and peer-reviewed health research. Data is current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.
Yes. Many medications (diuretics, beta-blockers, ACE inhibitors) affect sodium balance. Always inform your doctor of your medications.
